_comment: Copy this file and edit the paths to point at the proteomes on your system. Each entry needs name (short identifier), path (absolute or relative FASTA path), and category (apicomplexan or background). The apicomplexan entries define the positive-control distribution (real invasion machinery); the background entries define the free-living baseline (no invasion). A good panel has at least 3 apicomplexan and 3 background proteomes.

name: Toxoplasma_gondii
notes: Gold standard apicomplexan with classical invasion machinery. Source of training data.

name: Plasmodium_falciparum
notes: Haematozoan; close apicomplexan with distinct AMA/RON complex.

name: Cryptosporidium_parvum
notes: Reduced apicomplexan lineage; fewer compartments but retains core invasion.

name: Babesia_microti
notes: Piroplasm; intracellular apicomplexan with reduced invasion machinery.

name: Chromera_velia
notes: Free-living photosynthetic chrompodellid. Sister lineage to Apicomplexa, has conoid but no invasion machinery.

name: Vitrella_brassicaformis
notes: Free-living chrompodellid; close outgroup to Apicomplexa.

name: Voromonas_pontica
notes: Predatory chrompodellid; has an invasion-like feeding apparatus but not an apicomplexan-style one.

name: Tetrahymena_thermophila
notes: Ciliate. Distant alveolate, free-living, no invasion machinery. Should look like biological background for invasion prediction.
